meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Al Jazeera reports from Strait of Hormuz on Iran’s plan to manage shipping
items.summary: Al Jazeera’s Tohid Asadi reports from the strait and explains Tehran’s newest plan to manage shipping.

items.title: Bulgaria wins Eurovision for first time; Israel comes second amid boycott
items.summary: Spain, Netherlands, Ireland, Iceland and Slovenia stage biggest political boycott in Eurovision history over Gaza war.

items.title: Sardar Azmoun left out as Iran announce World Cup 2026 squad
items.summary: Veteran striker Azmoun was not named in the 30-man squad, which will be trimmed to 26 by June 1.

items.title: Federal funding for Trump ballroom in trouble after Senate ruling
items.summary: Republicans seek to allocate $1bn in public funds for Secret Service security upgrades linked to the plans.

items.title: Chelsea appoint Xabi Alonso as new manager on four-year deal
items.summary: Former Real Madrid coach replaces Liam Rosenior at the end of a difficult season for the Premier League club.

items.title: Modest fashion’s global turn
items.summary: From Instagram to Paris catwalks, modest fashion is booming as the global Muslim market heads toward $433bn by 2028.

items.title: India’s Tata and Dutch giant ASML sign semiconductor deal during Modi visit
items.summary: Prime Minister Modi says his talks with the Dutch PM also focused on expanding cooperation in defence and security.

items.title: Could a leadership change undo Israel’s international isolation?
items.summary: Opposition leaders hope to end Israel's isolation, even as they retain policies towards Palestinians that led to it.

items.title: Iran war day 79: Tehran to unveil Hormuz toll plan; Israel bombs Lebanon
items.summary: Iran says it will soon reveal the plan as Trump warns Tehran it will have a 'very bad time' if deal not reached soon.

items.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, Uganda a global health emergency
items.summary: DR Congo accounts for all except two of the suspected cases, both of which were reported in neighbouring Uganda.

items.title: FIFA holds ‘positive’ talks with Iranian football officials on World Cup
items.summary: FIFA offers solutions for each of Iran's concerns before games in US, Iran's football chief says.

items.title: ‘Timmy’ the rescued humpback whale confirmed dead
items.summary: ‘Timmy’ the rescued humpback whale confirmed dead, found off Danish coast.

items.title: Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano fight: Rousey wins with a 17-second submission
items.summary: Rousey won the fight with her signature armbar lock, forcing Carano to submit just 17 seconds into the bout.

items.title: Algeria’s USM Alger beat Egypt’s Zamalek to win CAF Cup
items.summary: Algeria's USM Alger beat Egypt's Zamalek to win CAF Cup.

items.title: Activists troll far-right UK rally with giant pro-immigration clip
items.summary: Led By Donkeys has snuck a big screen streaming pro-immigration messages into a far-right rally in the UK.

items.title: Tunisians rally amid economic crisis and political arrests
items.summary: Hundreds of Tunisians have marched through the capital to denounce a worsening economic crisis and crackdown on dissent.

items.title: Iraq’s new PM Ali al-Zaidi formally takes over
items.summary: Iraq’s new prime minister Ali al‑Zaidi has formally taken office in Baghdad, pledging reforms.

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran plans tolls in Hormuz; Trump warns of ‘very bad time’
items.summary: Iran to reveal its plan for Strait of Hormuz soon as Israel attacks Lebanon and Gaza, killing and wounding dozens.

items.title: Bolivia launches early-morning crackdown on roadblocks outside La Paz
items.summary: The government of Rodrigo Paz has sent about 3,500 soldiers and police to clear protesters demanding his resignation.

items.title: German police assault protesters at Nakba anniversary rally
items.summary: German police hit and pepper-spray protesters as thousands gathered in Berlin to mark the 78th anniversary of the Nakba.

items.title: Two presidential campaign staffers killed in Colombia as elections near
items.summary: Rights office says the violence could hinder 'exercise of political rights' ahead of May vote to replace Gustavo Petro.

items.title: Poll shows Lula and Bolsonaro tied before Brazil’s presidential election
items.summary: Right-wing challenger Flavio Bolsonaro faces new scrutiny over a film funding scandal, which could affect the race.

items.title: Taiwan says it is an independent nation after Trump arms sale remarks
items.summary: Taiwan stressed that it is a “sovereign and independent” nation

items.title: Bolivian army attempts to clear roads after 11 days of protests
items.summary: Military police in Bolivia arrested demonstrators, and used tear gas to disrupt road blockades after 11 days of protests

items.title: Thousands protest at Eurovision final as five countries boycott over Israel
items.summary: Spain, Ireland, Iceland, Slovenia and Netherlands all withdrew in protest against Israel's participation amid Gaza war.
